name.fr: Gua bao (petit pain vapeur plié taïwanais)

description.en: Taiwan's answer to the sandwich: a flat, faintly sweet steamed bun is folded like a coin purse around braised pork belly, pickled mustard greens and crushed peanuts. Steamed, not baked, so it stays soft enough to fold without ever cracking.
description.de: Taiwans Antwort auf das Sandwich: Ein flaches, leicht süßes Dampfbrötchen wird wie ein Geldbeutel um geschmorten Schweinebauch, eingelegtes Senfgemüse und gehackte Erdnüsse gefaltet. Gedämpft statt gebacken, bleibt es weich genug, um ohne zu reißen zu falten.
description.es: La respuesta de Taiwán al sándwich: un bollo plano y algo dulce, cocido al vapor, se pliega como un monedero alrededor de panceta de cerdo braseada, mostaza china encurtida y maní triturado. Al vapor, no horneado, queda lo bastante blando para plegarse sin romperse.
description.fr: La réponse de Taïwan au sandwich : un petit pain plat et légèrement sucré, cuit à la vapeur, est plié comme un porte-monnaie autour de poitrine de porc braisée, de légumes moutarde marinés et de cacahuètes concassées. Cuit vapeur, il plie sans jamais craquer.

origin.note: Brought by Fuzhounese immigrants and popularized as street food from the 1970s; it became far better known abroad after chef David Chang's Momofuku popularized it as the 'pork belly bun' in the 2000s.

label.es: panceta de cerdo braseada, en lonchas

ingredients.note: traditional filling, braised separately and folded in at serving

label.en: pickled mustard greens and crushed peanuts
label.es: mostaza china encurtida y maní triturado
label.fr: légumes moutarde marinés et cacahuètes concassées

yield.shape: folded oval steamed bun (bun only, unfilled)

keeping.note: The plain steamed bun freezes and resteams beautifully; classically assembled fresh with hot braised pork at serving.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Combine flour, yeast, baking powder and sugar, then work in water to a smooth, soft dough.

method.title: Rise
method.summary: Let the dough rise until noticeably puffy.

method.title: Divide and shape
method.summary: Divide into pieces, flatten each into an oval, brush lightly with oil, and fold in half with a piece of parchment inside so the fold won't seal shut.

method.title: Proof
method.summary: Rest the folded buns about 30 minutes until slightly enlarged.

method.title: Steam
method.summary: Steam over a full boil for about 10 minutes.

method.title: Fill
method.summary: Prise open the warm bun and fill with braised pork belly, pickled mustard greens and crushed peanuts.

source.adaptation: Bun dough grams kept as sourced; a short bulk rise was added as standard practice since the source's steps jumped straight to shaping; pork and garnish quantities are a typical per-bun estimate.
